title: Tasty Vietnamese Cherry Terrine
description: A funky, gooey, festive dessert that combines fresh cherries with a silky lacquer glaze, all served in individual mini cocottes.
instructions: In a saucepan, combine coconut milk, erythritol, stevia, soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, garlic powder, ginger powder, and salt; heat until the sweeteners dissolve.
Remove from heat and stir in melted dark chocolate and vanilla extract until the mixture is smooth.
Place pitted cherries into each mini cocotte, filling about one‑third full.
Pour the glaze mixture over the cherries, ensuring even coverage.
Tap each cocotte gently to release any trapped air bubbles.
Cover each cocotte with a lid or a small foil dome to retain moisture.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the glaze thickens and the cherries soften.
Remove from oven and let the terrines cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve warm or chilled, optionally dusted with a light sprinkle of cocoa powder.
